ticket.title: CMS FE: Form Field Styling - Text, Text Area (Reskin)

summary: Reskin the Text Field and Text Area form field components for the Global Atlantic design system. Fields must respond to the parent section's background (light vs dark mode) and implement all interactive, filled, and error states per the Figma design specifications.

user_stories.story: As a site visitor, I want Text Field and Text Area form components to visually adapt to their parent section background (white/slate = light mode, azul/granite = dark mode) so that form fields feel native to the page design.

As a site visitor, I want form fields to clearly indicate interactive states (Default, Hover, Focus, Filled, Disabled/Read Only) and error states (Error Default, Error Focus, Error Filled) so that I can understand how to interact with them and what errors have occurred.

As a user with accessibility needs, I want all form field text to meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast requirements in both light and dark mode, and disabled fields to not be keyboard-focusable, so that the forms are accessible.
As a mobile user, I want form field components to span the full width of their container and maintain consistent font styles with the desktop experience.
As a content author, I want updated authoring documentation and style guide pages with all variations so that I can configure form fields correctly in AEM.

all_components: All five interactive states are reskinned per Figma: Default, Hover, Focus, Filled, Disabled/Read Only
All three error states are reskinned per Figma: Error Default, Error Focus, Error Filled
Dark mode variants are implemented for all states across both components
Light and dark mode styles do not interfere with each other
Font styles (size, weight, family) are identical between light and dark mode — color only changes
All text meets WCAG 2.1 AA contrast in both light and dark mode
Mobile: components span full width of their container; font styles match desktop
text_field: Label renders above the field; required fields display an asterisk (*)
Helper text renders below the field
Error state shows error icon + message below the field
Disabled/Read Only state is not keyboard-focusable
text_area: Character count (e.g., 0/240) renders in the top-right corner and updates live on input
delivery: Component should be available on any existing templates (except Rate Administration)
Authoring Guide should exist and be updated with style variations
Both desktop and mobile versions need to be implemented
qa_checklist: Notify the design team that the component is ready for their review and provide a link to the Style Guide page

components_out_of_scope: Search Field (to be handled separately with search component — per comment by Hemanth Ponnuru)

focus_state_specs.note: The two-border focus ring is intentional; inner gap is visually 2px, outer outline is visually 2px

text_field_specifics.error_display: error icon + message below the field

text_area_specifics.character_count: renders top-right corner (e.g., 0/240), updates live on input

responsive.mobile: components span full width of container
responsive.font_parity: font styles identical between mobile and desktop

aem_authoring.templates: available on all existing templates except Rate Administration
aem_authoring.authoring_guide: must be created or updated with style variations

technical_requirements.accessibility_tool: Level Access Extension (https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/level-access-extension/kgbmnemfaellbfabmkmmilchbhiigpdi) — no critical or major issues permitted

non_functional_requirements.requirement: WCAG 2.1 AA contrast compliance for all text in both light and dark mode
Disabled/Read Only state must not be keyboard-focusable
Zero critical or major issues flagged by the Level Access Extension
Full-width layout on mobile; consistent font styles between mobile and desktop
Light and dark mode styles must not interfere with each other
Font size, weight, and family must remain identical across light and dark mode — only color values change
All styles must match the Figma design specification

open_questions.author: Sai Ganesh
open_questions.question: If helper text should appear below the text box alongside an error message, the Figma design needs to show both simultaneously so that correct spacing between them can be implemented.

open_questions.author: Marianne Embree
open_questions.question: An OOB error flag/message from the original KKR/Activate implementation is rendering unexpectedly in the form field. Design was unaware of this behavior and wants it removed. Decision needed: update this ticket or create a follow-up.
open_questions.status: open — Vivek Ranjan Kumar / Sai Ganesh / team to decide

dependencies.title: CMS FE: Form Field Styling - Dropdown (Reskin)
dependencies.note: Dropdown reskin is a parallel clone of this story; same pattern should be applied consistently

figma.note: Figma link referenced in ticket description as 'the figma link below' but not stored as a structured URL in Jira. Design specs were communicated via Jira comments (e.g., focus state box-shadow values confirmed by Marianne Embree on 2026-03-12).

attachment.description: Screenshot showing OOB error flag behavior in KKR that design wants removed

comments_summary.author: Hemanth Ponnuru
comments_summary.note: Search fields removed from scope — must be handled with the search component, not form fields.

comments_summary.author: Marianne Embree
comments_summary.note: Confirmed: Search field removed from story description.

comments_summary.author: Vivek Ranjan Kumar
comments_summary.note: Confirmed removal of Search Field from description text as well.

comments_summary.author: Marianne Embree
comments_summary.note: Clarified two-border focus ring is intentional. Light BG: box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #FFF, 0 0 0 4px #154197. Dark BG: box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #1A273C, 0 0 0 4px #FFF.

comments_summary.author: Sai Ganesh
comments_summary.note: Raised need for Figma to show helper text + error message simultaneously for proper spacing implementation.

comments_summary.author: Marianne Embree
comments_summary.note: Flagged OOB error flag from KKR/Activate appearing in form field rendering — design wants it removed. Team to decide: update this ticket or create follow-up.
